title: The 27 Flavour Board
ask: One static post showing all 27 khakhra flavours. Methi is the captain, the rest are the team. The queue should be able to read it from across the road.

title: No Discount, Full Respect
ask: A static announcing that prices are not changing. That is the entire news. Make a price NOT changing feel like an event.
finePrint: This is a plain announcement post. Anyone trying to make it 'interesting' gets a call from the family.

title: Queue at 7 AM
ask: A reel of the morning queue. Aunties, office-goers, one confused tourist. The khakhra leaves the counter still warm and so should the edit.
finePrint: The queue must look fast and joyful. No slow motion, slow queues scare customers.

title: Khakhra Caption Pack
ask: Ten captions for the month. Tone: your grandmother, if your grandmother were extremely confident on the internet.

title: Butter Hero Shot
ask: One static of the vadapav with butter that looks like a lifestyle choice. The pav glistens. The vada commands respect.

title: New Branch Poster
ask: Opening poster for the new branch. The vadapav should look bigger than the building. The building is three storeys. Do the needful.
finePrint: Six cousins approve this poster. Feedback arrives in waves. Lock a version early or drown.

title: Tawa Sizzle Reel
ask: A reel of the tawa at full chaos. Butter hits, steam rises, hands move like a card trick. Hunger in the first half second.
finePrint: No meme audio. The cousin committee voted on this. It was unanimous, eventually.

title: Menu Board Rewrite
ask: Rewrite the names of 12 menu items so each one sounds like a legend without changing what it is. The cheese vadapav has notions. Honour them.

title: Sunday Khaman Ritual
ask: A static for Sunday morning. Khaman, sev, green chutney, one beam of sunlight that has clearly been arranged in advance.

title: Steam Don't Stop
ask: A reel of fresh khaman coming off the steamer. The steam is the celebrity. Everything else is supporting cast.
finePrint: Fast cuts only. In slow motion the khaman looks cold by the end and Uncle ji will notice.

title: Softness Manifesto
ask: Write five lines about softness. The khaman is soft. The family is soft. The lines should make a grown man call his mother.
finePrint: Heritage tone only. Anything trending dies at this counter.

title: Festival Counter Rush
ask: The festival rush announcement. Timings, pre-orders, a plea for patience. Whatever format gets it out the door fastest.
finePrint: Needed before tomorrow's first batch. The first batch is at 6 AM. The 6 is not flexible.

title: New Flavour: Top Secret
ask: Key art for a flavour launch where marketing refuses to reveal the flavour. Design a chip reveal around a chip you cannot fully show. Welcome to wafers.
finePrint: The flavour is under NDA until launch day. One leaked hint and legal calls before the chip cools.

title: Family Pack Pyramid
ask: A static of the full range stacked into a pyramid. Not one chip. A system of chips. The marketing head was very clear about the system.

title: Crunch ASMR
ask: A reel where the crunch is the entire story. Build to the bite. The bite is the drop. The wafer is the artist.
finePrint: The crunch must read on mute. Captions, waveforms, whatever it takes.
No trending audio. The crunch IS the audio.

title: Name Six Flavours
ask: Naming for the new namkeen line. Six names that sound delicious in Gujarati, Hindi, and to a distributor reading them off a carton at 5 AM.
finePrint: Names get printed on lakhs of packets. Legal checks every word for accidental meanings in nine languages.

title: Morning Cup Static
ask: One static of the morning cup. Steam, a newspaper, the specific peace that exists before everyone else wakes up.

title: Steam With Dignity
ask: Cut the brand film down to 30 seconds. The chai is poured, the family gathers, nobody rushes. Except you. You rush.
finePrint: The founder family finds slow motion 'too filmy'. The steam rises slowly. The edit does not.

title: Chai Time Reel
ask: A reel that makes a 22-year-old put down the cold coffee. Young energy, same old chai. The brand book is watching.
finePrint: Young, but no memes. Everything gets reviewed at the family's Sunday breakfast and memes do not survive breakfast.

title: Generations of Chai
ask: Write lines about generations sharing chai without using the word legacy. They are tired of the word legacy. The word legacy is on a list.
finePrint: This is a tribute post, not a trend post. Keep it timeless or keep it to yourself.

title: Summer War Key Visual
ask: The key visual for summer. Every flavour reporting for duty. The sun is the villain. The cart is the cavalry.
finePrint: Due before the heatwave peaks. The heatwave peaks tomorrow. The weather report is the project plan.

title: Kulfi Hero Poster
ask: Poster for the kulfi range. The kulfi gets the lighting of a 200 crore movie hero. Entry shot energy, dessert format.

title: Melt Test Reel
ask: A reel of scoops in full summer glory. The catch: the ice cream can never appear melted in any frame. Edit around physics.
finePrint: One pass only. The scoop budget is finished. No reshoots, no endless polish rounds.

title: Cart Rush Shoot
ask: Shoot kids pulling their parents toward the cart. Real carts, real kids, real heat. Capture the pulling. The pulling is the brand. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: Product melts in four minutes under this sun. Every shot is scheduled like a heist.

title: One Glass Vacation
ask: A static where one glass of orange looks like an entire summer holiday. The condensation does the heavy lifting.

title: 90s Summer Rewind
ask: A nostalgia montage. Power cuts, cousins, steel glasses, one orange pitcher in the middle of it all. Make millennials feel personally attacked, lovingly.
finePrint: Pure nostalgia. One trending transition and the whole feeling dies on the table.

title: Tagline for Two Generations
ask: One line that lands on a parent who grew up with it and a kid who has never mixed it. Two hearts, one tagline.
finePrint: The old jingle is off limits. Rights reasons. Legal was very calm and very clear.

title: Mango Launch Blitz
ask: Everything for mango flavour day one. Static, story, caption, chaos. Whoever is free, make it land.
finePrint: Launch day is fixed. The mangoes are already ripe. Nothing moves.

title: Topical in Four Hours
ask: The news broke this morning. They want the take by evening. One static, one pun, a nation watching. No pressure.
finePrint: Due before the news cycle turns. You have hours, not days.
Witty, never mean. One offended community and everyone in this office retires together.

title: Butter on Everything
ask: A campaign static where butter improves an everyday situation. Breakfast, traffic, a Monday. Pick a misery, apply butter.

title: Breakfast Anthem Cutdown
ask: Cut the 60 second breakfast film to 15 seconds. The jingle is 22 seconds long. They see no contradiction in this. Find one cut and ship it.
finePrint: The jingle is untouchable. One approved cut, no extra polish rounds, the slot is booked.

title: Utterly Punny Captions
ask: A week of pun captions. Seven days, seven groans, seven shares. The pun standard here is generational, write accordingly.
finePrint: Puns, not memes. There is a difference and the brand IS the difference.

title: Midnight Notification Pack
ask: Twenty push notifications for the midnight craving window. Thirteen words max each. Make insomnia profitable.
finePrint: Every line ships to millions of phones. Legal reads all twenty for slang that means something else somewhere.

title: Rainy Day Push Copy
ask: It is raining. Five notifications that turn weather into orders. Pakora is implied, never stated. That is the art.

title: App Banner: Biryani Day
ask: The in-app banner for Biryani Day. The rice should look individually blessed. Tap-through is the only review metric.
finePrint: The biryani must look exactly like the biryani that arrives. Expectation lawsuits route through us, then to you.

title: Craving in Three Seconds
ask: A 15 second reel that creates hunger by second three. Cheese pull, steam, the exact sound of a delivery bag opening.
finePrint: First bite lands by second three. Slow motion pushes it to second nine. Do the math.

title: Pink Friday Master Visual
ask: The key visual for the biggest sale of the quarter. One image, four thousand products implied, zero products cluttering it.
finePrint: The pink is a specific pink. Close enough is a resignation letter.
Sale assets lock tonight. There is no second pass.

title: Shade 47 Swatch Reel
ask: A swatch reel for the new lipstick line. Shade 47 and shade 48 must be visibly different on a 2018 phone in sunlight. This is the whole job.

title: GRWM Cutdown Marathon
ask: Nine creator videos arrive in nine formats. Cut them into one launch reel that looks like it was always one video.

title: Festive Sale Shoot
ask: Tabletop shoot for the festive sale. Sixty products, one day, lighting that makes a kajal pencil look like jewellery. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: Sixty products, one day, zero overtime budget. The schedule is the creative. Endless retakes sink it.

title: Mountain Poster
ask: A poster of the bike on a mountain pass. No people, no copy, no mercy. The bike and the silence do the talking.
finePrint: No neon, no gradients, no trends. If it would not work in 1995, it does not work now.

title: The Thump Edit
ask: Cut the ride film with the engine as the entire soundtrack. The thump sets the tempo. You follow the thump.
finePrint: Trending audio on the thump is grounds for ending the contract. They have done it before.

title: Dawn Ride Shoot
ask: Shoot the new model at a mountain pass at dawn. The light, the mist, one rider who has clearly earned this moment. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: Usable light exists from 5:50 to 6:20. Miss the window, wait a full day, eat the cost.

title: Workshop Stories
ask: A garage shoot. Hands, grease, tools, the bike mid-surgery. Honest and unhurried, like the brand pretends to be about deadlines. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)

title: Drop Day Lockscreen Teaser
ask: A teaser static for the new earbuds drop. Sneaker-launch energy, lockscreen-wallpaper quality, samosa price point visible.
finePrint: Drop is tomorrow 6 PM. The countdown is already public. There is no moving it.

title: Bass Test Reel
ask: A reel where the bass is visible. Water jumping on a speaker, screen shake, the works. The thumbnail alone should feel loud.

title: Campus Collab Shoot
ask: Shoot the earbuds at a college fest. Real crowd, real noise, the product surviving both. Lifestyle, fourteen times over. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: Venue permissions are 'in progress'. Shoot anyway, but keep every frame clean enough for legal to bless later.

title: Festival Mega Sale Blitz
ask: The festival sale bundle: statics, stories, one reel, captions. Whoever has hands free, this is theirs now.
finePrint: Everything ships together or nothing ships. One late asset sinks the whole bundle.

title: The Unbreakable Bench
ask: A wordless static. A bench, too many people on it, the bench unbothered. The bond is the punchline. The bond is always the punchline.

title: Bond Film 15s
ask: A 15 second visual gag. Something that should come apart, refusing to come apart, at the least convenient moment for everyone involved.
finePrint: The joke is timing. Slow motion murders timing. Test it on mute, then test it on uncles.

title: Decades of Stuck
ask: Lines celebrating decades of jokes about things that never come apart. Funny to a carpenter and a CEO in the same second.
finePrint: Heritage humour only. Trending formats expire. Fevicol does not.

title: Impossible Chair Shoot
ask: Shoot the chair stunt for real. No VFX, the chair genuinely holds, the crowd genuinely gasps. Practical comedy, practical glue. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: No VFX allowed. A safety officer signs off on every setup. Skipping the paperwork ends the shoot.

title: Golden Hour Dune Shoot
ask: Shoot the campaign stills on the dunes at golden hour. The sand performs once a day. Everyone else adjusts. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: Golden hour, not silver hour. The brand can tell the difference from a moving car. The window is 40 minutes.

title: Crew Lifestyle Shoot
ask: A lifestyle shoot around the crew experience. Everything in frame must be premium. The water. The light. The air, somehow. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: One shoot day, booked to the minute. Premium does not mean slow. Re-staging every setup burns the day.

title: First Class Film Cut
ask: Cut the first class film. Slow is fine. Cheap is not. Every transition should feel like a seat reclining into a bed.
finePrint: Luxury whispers. Any cut faster than a heartbeat is too loud.
No memes. Not even ironic ones. Especially not ironic ones.

title: Lounge Menu Elegance
ask: A static for the lounge dining menu. Beige variant 17, gold accents, food that looks like it has a passport.

title: Taller Than Yesterday
ask: Launch key visual for the new tower. It must look taller than the render. The render is already taller than the tower. Nobody acknowledges this.
finePrint: The sky must be the approved sky colour, which is not blue blue. There is a swatch. Learn the swatch.

title: Fountain Show Shoot
ask: Shoot the fountain show for the destination film. Water, lights, tourists gasping in eleven languages. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: The fountain runs on its schedule, not yours. Two passes per evening. That is all anyone gets.

title: Skyline Timelapse Film
ask: Cut a skyline timelapse film from three years of footage. Day to night, crane to tower, ambition to invoice.

title: Iconic by Tuesday
ask: Full launch support for the new district. Statics, film, copy, all of it. The brief is one line: make it iconic by Tuesday.
finePrint: Tuesday. The chairman's office said Tuesday. Nobody asks which Tuesday twice.

title: Iftar Countdown Creative
ask: The iftar countdown creative. The food appears the second the sun disappears. Warm, generous, perfectly timed.
finePrint: Goes live at the exact iftar minute, which changes daily. The schedule is astronomy. Astronomy does not negotiate.

title: Hunger in Two Languages
ask: Cut the campaign film in Arabic and English. Both versions must be equally funny. Equally is being measured.
finePrint: One review round per language. Endless polishing breaks the launch window in both alphabets.

title: Late Night Craving Copy
ask: Push copy for the 1 AM crowd. Short lines, deep hunger, zero judgement. The shawarma understands.

title: Rider POV Shoot
ask: A rider point-of-view shoot through the city at dusk. Orange box, golden light, a city that orders dinner like clockwork. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: Shoot permits cover exactly three streets. Stay on those three streets. Legal has a map and a temper.

title: Just Do It. But Different.
ask: The entire brief from the client, in full: 'Just do it. But different.' That is everything they sent. Write the campaign line.
finePrint: No explanations allowed in the deck. The line stands alone or it dies alone.

title: The Unwatched Athlete
ask: Shoot a film about an athlete nobody is watching. 4 AM training, empty stands, real sweat. If it looks like an ad, it failed. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: No gloss. If it looks staged, reshoot. The budget says you will not be reshooting.

title: Sixty Seconds of Truth
ask: Cut the anthem film. Sixty seconds, no voiceover crutch, no tricks. It has to hold a phone audience using nothing but truth and pacing.
finePrint: No trending sounds, no fast hooks. Let it breathe and still keep every thumb off the scroll.

title: Key Art, No Spoilers
ask: The poster for the new season. It must sell episode eight using nothing from episodes two through eight. This is considered normal here.
finePrint: Legal cleared exactly four frames for use. Use only those four. The fifth frame is a lawsuit.
One frame of spoiler and three time zones schedule meetings about you.

title: Trailer Tease Cut
ask: Cut the teaser trailer. Tease everything, reveal nothing, and land the climax exactly on the tudum.
finePrint: The tudum does not move for anyone. Time the cut to it.
No lingering slow motion. A trailer that drags gets skipped, and skips get reported upward.

title: Premiere Night Content Shoot
ask: Shoot the premiere night content. Red carpet, reactions, one perfect loop for socials. Shoot tonight, edit tonight, post tonight. (Production shoots it; the edit is a separate hand-off to your editors.)
finePrint: The premiere does not reschedule. Everything ships before the after-party ends.
